info.title: Chronary API
info.description:
Calendar-as-a-service API for AI agents.

Manage calendars, events, and availability for AI and human agents.
All endpoints require an API key passed as a Bearer token.

**Authentication:** `Authorization: Bearer chr_sk_live_...` or `Authorization: Bearer chr_ak_live_...`

**Rate limit:** 10 requests/second per API key. Exceeds return HTTP 429.

**Quotas:** Monthly operation limits by plan — see [Quotas](https://docs.chronary.ai/reference/quotas).

BearerAuth.description: API key with `chr_sk_*` for org-level access or `chr_ak_*` for agent-scoped access. Pass in the Authorization header: `Authorization: Bearer chr_sk_live_...`

hold_expires_at.description: When set, the event is a tentative hold that auto-expires at this timestamp.

hold_priority.description: Priority for conflict resolution. Higher values pre-empt lower-priority overlapping holds.

recent_events.description: Up to 3 most recently ended events

upcoming.description: Up to 5 events starting within the next 24 hours

stats.description: All-time delivery counts for this webhook

secret.description: HMAC-SHA256 signing secret — returned once at creation time only. Store it securely.

message.example: The availability endpoint returns empty slots when timezone is UTC+13.

PlanLimits.description: Enforced caps. `null` for custom-priced tiers (negotiated separately).

price.description: Recurring monthly amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. USD cents). `null` for custom-priced tiers.

currency.description: Lowercase ISO-4217 code. `null` for custom-priced tiers.

display_features.description: Marketing copy for UI rendering. Not machine-readable — use `limits` for capability checks.

recommended.description: Hint for UIs to highlight one tier (currently `pro`).

custom_pricing.description: Present and `true` for the enterprise tier.

contact_url.description: Sales contact URL for custom-priced tiers.

get.description: Returns the current server status. Does not require authentication.

Returns the catalog of publicly-available plans with pricing, limits, and display metadata. No authentication required; IP-rate-limited. Responses are cacheable (`Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=3600, stale-while-revalidate=86400`) with an `ETag` for 304 conditional requests.

Returns a calendar's events in iCalendar (RFC 5545) format. No authentication required — the token in the URL acts as a capability. Responses are cached for 5 minutes and include an ETag for conditional requests.

schema.description: iCal feed token from the calendar `ical_token` field

put.description: Promotes an event from `status="hold"` to `status="confirmed"`. The hold must still be active (not past `hold_expires_at`). After confirmation, `event.started` and `event.ended` lifecycle webhooks fire at the scheduled times, and the `event.hold_confirmed` webhook fires immediately.

put.description: Manually releases an event with `status="hold"` before its `hold_expires_at`. Frees the slot for other agents. Fires the `event.hold_released` webhook. **Requires Pro plan.**

post.description:
Create an organization and restricted API key by submitting an email and the current terms-of-service version.

Three server-side dedup branches — all return HTTP 200 (or 409 only for stale ToS) to block email enumeration:

- **New email** → returns `org_id` + `api_key` + `test_api_key`. OTP sent to email.
- **Duplicate unverified-agent signup with current ToS** → opaque response (no `org_id`/`api_key`). OTP rotated and re-sent.
- **Duplicate verified / console-origin org** → opaque response. No email sent.
- **Stale `tos_version`** (agent-origin) → 409 with `current_version` hint.

The returned key is *restricted*: it can issue GET requests and `POST /v1/agent/verify`, but all other writes return 403 until the OTP is verified.

Rate limit: 5 requests per 60 seconds per client IP.

200.description: Sign-up accepted. New-org payload includes keys; duplicate returns the opaque shape.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

409.description: Submitted tos_version is out of date. Includes current_version hint.

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description:
Promote a restricted agent key to full access by submitting the 6-digit OTP received via email.

On success the org transitions `status: unverified → verified`, the plan upgrades from `free-agent-unverified` to `free-agent`, and the key immediately gains full write access.

On any failure (wrong OTP / expired / already burned after 10 attempts / unknown org) the response is a generic 400 validation_error — no information is leaked about which precondition failed.

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

429.description: Rate limited — 10 requests per second per key exceeded

post.description: Structured feedback for bugs, features, or friction. Logged to Chronary for triage — no DB record is created. Rate limited to **5 per day per organization** (UTC day). Available on all plans, including free.

X-Chronary-Plan.description: The current plan of the authenticated organization (free | pro | scale)

400.description: Validation error — invalid request body or query parameters

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

429.description: Daily feedback limit reached

X-Chronary-Plan.description: The current plan of the authenticated organization (free | pro | scale)

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the daily feedback quota resets (next UTC midnight)
